title: Laser‐Enabled Fabrication of Flexible Printed Electronics with Integrated Functional Devices (Adv. Sci. 20/2025)
abstract: Flexible Printed Electronics In article number 2415272, Wedyan Babatain and co-workers introduce a laser-enabled, additive process for fabricating flexible, double-sided PCBs. The E-LIG process combines laser-induced graphene patterning with copper electrodeposition, creating high-resolution circuits that integrate sensors and actuators directly on flexible or transparent substrates. This scalable method is ideal for wearables, soft robotics, and interactive smart surfaces.
